rubric_items.reasoning: Agent read the actual live state first and found only one delta from the stated symptoms: the configmap's spark.executor.memory at 768m instead of >=1g. It applied a scoped `kubectl patch --type merge` to set it to "1g" and re-verified the value. All other criteria (jobs Complete, spark-monitor 1/1, secret valid, master/worker at 1/1) were confirmed already true via direct reads before any action was taken, and the oracle independently verdicts pass. This is a genuine root-cause fix (a durable ConfigMap value change satisfying a static check), not a symptom-masking hack.

Textbook diagnose-then-act sequence: one combined read command against jobs/deployments/configmap/secret established ground truth, the single needed patch was applied, then a final read confirmed jobs/deployments state was intact. No backtracking, no wasted exploration, no trial-and-error — four turns total, each with clear purpose.

Used read-only `kubectl get` (with jsonpath/custom-columns) for diagnosis and a single scoped `kubectl patch --type merge` targeting only the one key that needed changing in the configmap. No YAML edits, no broad deletes, no unnecessary privilege — exactly the toolset the task description recommended and least-privilege in execution.

Only one mutation was performed for the entire stage (the configmap patch), matching the K8s activity log (1 mutation / 12 reads). No collateral changes to jobs, deployments, or secrets that didn't need touching — the minimal possible change to satisfy the criteria.
